1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 080 — **three substantive prover lanes**, two with high-confidence closure paths, one experimental).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete, C1 scheduled iter-081+, C2 iter-082+, C3 iter-085+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-079 was the third substantive-progress iteration in a row (net 15 → 14 sorries; -1). Highlights:
20	- `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` (Modules/Monoidal.lean) closed via `CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal` with the architecture `sheafificationFunctor X` + `W X` + `instMonoidalCategoryPresheaf` (top-level bridging instance). Substantive content concentrated in the single permitted gap-fill `instIsMonoidal_W` (sorry retained).
21	- `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill landed fully closed (7 LOC) in Differentials.lean. This is one of two permitted gap-fills for `cotangentExactSeq_structure`; the second (`exact_iff_stalkwise`) is iter-080 Lane 2's responsibility.
22	- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` got a `change` step exposing the RHS smul as `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j` (defeq); identified the concrete unblock as a named-per-i refactor of `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃`.
23	
24	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
25	
26	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-080 plan |
27	|---|---|---|---|---|
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: named-per-i refactor of `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃` + complete S2–S8 to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: Route A per-section route + introduce second permitted gap-fill `exact_iff_stalkwise` (sorry body authorised; close `_structure` body net-0)** |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | **Lane 3 (experimental): stalks-level argument for `instIsMonoidal_W`; failure acceptable** |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-085+) |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
35	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
36	
37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
40	
41	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — refactor `h_mod_pi_n` + close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
42	
43	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1145 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1185 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1214 `h_loc_exact`.
44	
45	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1145), the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack. The iter-079 prover delivered a precise actionable unblock: refactor `h_mod_pi₁`, `h_mod_pi₂`, `h_mod_pi₃` from anonymous `Pi.module` builders to named per-i builders, then `Pi.smul_apply` fires and the S2–S8 chain proceeds.
46	
47	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** Three iter-078/079 commits are essential prerequisites and must be preserved:
48	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 above the docstring of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` (iter-078). Required because *any* tactic step inside the `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block triggers `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose whnf cost exceeds 200000 at the theorem head.
49	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1093–1094 (iter-078). Cleanly reduces the goal to the componentwise form.
50	- `change e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r (e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y))) j` at L1115–1118 (iter-079). Closes by defeq, exposes the RHS smul as `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j` explicitly (sidesteps `simp [Pi.smul_apply]` failures).
51	
52	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map.
53	
54	**Recipe.**
55	
56	1. **Refactor `h_mod_pi₂` at L921–930** to use named per-i instance:
57	   ```lean
58	   -- BEFORE:
59	   have h_mod_pi₂ : Module R (∀ i, Z₂ i) :=
60	     @Pi.module (Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) (fun i => Z₂ i) R _ _ (fun i => by
61	       apply RingHom.toModule
62	       refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
63	       let a0 : Fin (n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
64	       have h1 : ∏ᶜ … ≤ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
65	       have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
66	         basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
67	       exact h1.trans h2)
68	   -- AFTER:
69	   letI perI₂ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₂ i) := fun i => by
70	     apply RingHom.toModule
71	     refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
72	     let a0 : Fin (n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
73	     have h1 : ∏ᶜ … ≤ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
74	     have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
75	       basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
76	     exact h1.trans h2
77	   have h_mod_pi₂ : Module R (∀ i, Z₂ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
78	   ```
79	   Apply symmetrically to `h_mod_pi₁` (L911, `perI₁` over `Fin (prev n + 1) → ↑s₀`) and `h_mod_pi₃` (L931, `perI₃` over `Fin (n + 2) → ↑s₀`). The semantic content of each `h_mod_pi_n` is unchanged byte-for-byte; only typeclass-visibility of the per-i builders changes. Per user policy 2026-05-11 this is **NOT a new project-local helper lemma** — it's a literal expansion of the existing `Pi.module` construction into named pieces.
80	2. **Verify** that after the refactor, the `change` step at L1115–1118 still elaborates (the RHS now naturally exposes `perI₂ j .toSMul.smul`-shape, which is what `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul` reduces to).
81	3. **Complete the S2–S8 chain** by extending the `change` step:
82	   - **S2 (5-layer dsimp).** `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]` (or the equivalent unfold chain) exposes `(scK₀.f).hom = objD X (prev n)` where `X := (evalOp.obj P) ∘ (mk _ cech).rightOp`, `P := (sheafToPresheaf _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
83	   - **S3 (objD identification).** Use `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` to expose the differential as `∑ k : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^k • X.δ k` where `X.δ k = X.map (op (SimplexCategory.δ k))`. Use `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` (or `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split) to handle the `K₀.d (prev n) n` opacity (the iter-076 surface).
84	   - **S4 (`Pi.smul_apply` on LHS).** With the named-per-i refactor in place, this now fires: evaluates `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) = perI₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) .smul r (y (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom))`.
85	   - **S5 (`Finset.smul_sum`).** Distribute `r •` across the alternating sum.
86	   - **S6 (`Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_`).** Reduce to per-summand R-linearity at fixed index `k`.
87	   - **S7 (per-summand).** For each `k`, the j-component of `X.δ k` is `Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ (P.map (φ_k j).op)` via `evalOp_obj_map` + `Pi.lift`. Per-component R-linearity follows from `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp` on the `V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k} ≤ U` chain.
88	   - **S8.** Close by `rfl` or `ring`.
89	4. Verify the file compiles and `h_diff_pi_smul_f` sorry count drops from 1 → 0 (file total: 6 → 5).
90	
91	**Approaches ruled out iter-076/078/079** (do NOT re-attempt):
92	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against the anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` without the refactor: "did not find the pattern". Confirmed iter-076, iter-078, iter-079.
93	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass `h_mod_pi₂` anonymity: `letI mod_Z₂_per : ∀ i, Module R (Z₂ i) := …` followed by `letI : ∀ i, SMul R (Z₂ i) := …` makes the per-i instance visible but `Pi.smul_apply` STILL fails because the smul in the goal is from `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul`, not the new `letI`, and Lean doesn't bridge the two Pi-builders at the instance level. Confirmed iter-079.
94	- `rfl` after the iter-079 `change`: LHS and RHS are NOT defeq without first running S2's `dsimp`.
95	- `show e₂ (…) j = r • (e₂ (…)) j`: HSMul synthesis fails on `Z₂ j` alone (no standalone R-action; only on the product via `h_mod_pi₂`).
96	- `exact (LinearEquiv.map_smul e₂ r _)`: `e₂` is `k`-linear, not `R`-linear.
97	- `show … = (Pi.instSMul.smul r _) j`: `Pi.instSMul` ≠ `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` syntactically.
98	- `change … = (@HSMul.hSMul _ _ _ Pi.instHSMul …)`: `Pi.instHSMul` is not a global identifier.
99	
100	**Fallback** if downstream uses of `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul` break after the refactor: keep both `perI₂` AND `h_mod_pi₂` in scope (parallel availability) and use the appropriate name at each use site. The `RingHom.toModule`-based fields of `h_mod_pi₂` remain bit-for-bit identical (the refactor only adds visibility, doesn't change content).
101	
102	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-078/079** (verify via `lean_local_search`):
103	- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map`
104	- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD`
105	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (`@[elementwise]`)
106	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` (`@[elementwise]`)
107	- Project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161) — the k-level version; the R-level analogue is what S7 needs.
108	- `Pi.smul_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Finset.sum_congr`, `RingHom.map_mul`, `presheaf.map_comp`.
109	
110	**Hard constraints**:
111	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). The `letI perI_n` refactor is a literal byte-for-byte expansion of the existing `Pi.module` construction, NOT a new helper.

5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps

15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures.

21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy

71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only

76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.

23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.

30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.

47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:

70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.

74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.

131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c
143	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
144	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
145	  refine {
146	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
147	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
148	  }
149	  case naturality =>
150	    ext x
151	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
152	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
153	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
154	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
155	      simpa using this
156	    exact d'.d_map g x

158	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
159	
160	The cotangent exact sequence
161	```
162	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
163	```
164	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
165	
166	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
167	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
168	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
169	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
170	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
171	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
172	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`
173	   that kills the `A`-linear derivations factoring through `A'`.
174	3. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha_comp_Beta` — composition zero `α ≫ β = 0`,
175	   from the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
176	   range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`).
177	4. `cotangentExactSeqShortComplexExact` — exactness `ker β = im α`,
178	   from the same ring-level theorem and gluing.
179	5. `cotangentExactSeqBetaEpi` — `β` is an epi, from
180	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` applied on each affine chart.
181	
182	The headline theorem `cotangent_exact_sequence` then assembles the
183	existential bundle from these five components without further sorries. -/

686	    -- Goal now: `<d1_inline>.d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`. By the inline definition of
687	    -- `d1` in `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (`{ d := d2.d, ... }` with `d2 := derivation' φ_2'`),
688	    -- the `.d` field projects to `d2.d = (derivation' φ_2').d` (rfl after structure
689	    -- projection). So the goal is `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, i.e.
690	    -- `hd_app`.
691	    exact hd_app
692	  · -- Sub-claim `h_exact` : the short complex is exact at the middle term.
693	    --
694	    -- Strategy.
695	    -- Same ring-level input as `h_zero`: `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`
696	    -- states `Function.Exact (mapBaseChange R A B) (map R A B B)`, i.e. range equals kernel.
697	    -- For the sheaf-of-modules short complex, exactness is checked stalkwise: the
698	    -- stalk functor on sheaves of modules over a scheme preserves and reflects exactness,
699	    -- and at each stalk `Ω_{X/S,x}`, the cotangent short complex specialises to the
700	    -- ring-level exact sequence applied to the local ring chain
701	    -- `O_{S,g(f(x))} → O_{Y,f(x)} → O_{X,x}`.
702	    --
703	    -- The full implementation requires either:
704	    --   (a) a project-local `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` helper (the natural
705	    --       analogue of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for stalks), or
706	    --   (b) routing through `ShortComplex.exact_iff_of_concrete_homology` with abelian-category
707	    --       infrastructure, or
708	    --   (c) building the exact-sequence witness directly from the ring-level Kähler exactness
709	    --       applied at each affine chart `A → A' → B`.
710	    -- Route (a) is the pragmatic choice. Left as `sorry` for the next iteration; the strategy
711	    -- + ring-level input are pinned.
712	    -- (sorry)
713	  · -- Sub-claim `h_epi` : `β` is an epimorphism.
714	    --
715	    -- Strategy.
716	    -- Local statement: on each affine chart, `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` is surjective
717	    -- (Mathlib's `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`, RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean L710).
718	    -- PresheafOfModules level: `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`
719	    -- (ModuleCat/Presheaf/EpiMono.lean L59) characterises epi as locally-surjective.
720	    -- SheafOfModules level: an `epi_iff_epi_presheaf` helper is required to bridge from the
721	    -- PresheafOfModules-level epi to the SheafOfModules-level epi. Mathlib does not provide
722	    -- this directly; a project-local lemma using the abstraction layer of `Sheafify` /
723	    -- conservativity is the pragmatic route.
724	    -- Left as `sorry` for the next iteration; the strategy + ring-level input are pinned.
725	    -- (sorry)
726	-/
727	
728	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
729	
730	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
731	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
732	```
733	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
734	```
735	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
736	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
737	compatibility above.
738	
739	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
740	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
741	itself is closed by assembling them.
742	
743	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
744	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
745	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
746	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
747	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
748	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
749	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
750	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
751	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
752	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
753	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
754	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
755	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
756	
757	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
758	
759	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
760	being locally free of the given rank.
761	
762	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
763	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
764	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
765	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
766	    Smooth f ↔
767	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
768	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
769	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
770	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
771	  sorry
772	
773	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
774	
775	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
776	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
777	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
778	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
779	`k`-vector space. -/
780	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
781	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
782	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
783	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
784	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
785	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
786	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)

790	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
791	
792	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
793	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
794	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
795	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
796	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
797	    (M : C.left.Modules)
798	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
799	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
800	
801	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
802	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
803	restriction of scalars. -/
804	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
805	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
806	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
807	    (M : C.left.Modules)
808	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
809	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
810	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
811	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
812	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
813	  -- naturality.
814	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
815	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
816	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
817	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
818	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
819	  -- Now goal:
820	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
821	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
822	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
823	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
824	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
825	    ext x
826	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
827	  rw [hpres]
828	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
829	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
830	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
831	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
832	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
833	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
834	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
835	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
836	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
837	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
838	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
839	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
840	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
841	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
842	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
843	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
844	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
845	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
846	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
847	    rw [halg]
848	  rw [htarget] at hnat
849	  exact hnat
850	
851	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
852	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
853	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
854	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
855	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
856	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
857	    (M : C.left.Modules)
858	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
859	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
860	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
861	
862	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
863	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
864	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
865	    (M : C.left.Modules)
866	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
867	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
868	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
869	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
870	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
871	
872	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
873	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
874	
875	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
876	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
877	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
878	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
879	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
880	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
881	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
882	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
883	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
884	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
885	  map_id U := by
886	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
887	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
888	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
889	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
890	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
891	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
892	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
893	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
894	
895	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
896	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
897	`O_C`-module. -/
898	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
899	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
900	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
901	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
902	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
903	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
904	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
905	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
906	
907	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
908	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
909	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
910	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
911	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
912	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
913	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
914	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
915	
916	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
917	
918	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
919	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
920	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
921	
922	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
923	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
924	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
925	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
926	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
927	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
928	      Module.rank k
929	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
930	  sorry
931	
932	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

Both `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` (L135) and `instMonoidalCategory` (L142) derive from this single sorry via `inferInstanceAs (LocalizedMonoidal …)`. `tensorObj` (L60) preserved byte-for-byte from iter-078.

141	**Why this is hard.** Three routes are documented as blocked (`task_done.md` iter-079 entry):
142	- (a) `tensorHom_def` decomposition reduces to `whiskerLeft`/`whiskerRight` — same condition, no progress.
143	- (b) `sheafificationCompToSheaf` route — `toPresheaf R₀` not monoidal because the R₀(U)-balanced presheaf-of-modules tensor ≠ the ℤ-tensor on `AddCommGrpCat`.
144	- (c) Closedness via internal hom — Mathlib has `MonoidalClosed` only for fixed-ring `ModuleCat R`, not varying-ring `PresheafOfModules R₀`. Multi-PR Mathlib upstream effort.
145	
146	**Recipe (stalks-level route, experimental).**
147	
148	1. **Unfold the goal** post-`simp only [MorphismProperty.inverseImage_iff, MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.iff] at hf hg ⊢`: `IsIso ((sheafificationFunctor X).map (f ⊗ₘ g))` with hypotheses `hf : IsIso (L.map f)`, `hg : IsIso (L.map g)`.
149	2. **Decompose via `tensorHom_def`** `f ⊗ₘ g = (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ (X₂ ◁ g)`. Closes if both `IsIso (L.map (f ▷ Y₁))` and `IsIso (L.map (X₂ ◁ g))` hold.
150	3. **Focus on one side**, say `IsIso (L.map (X₂ ◁ g))`. After sheafification, an iso is detected stalkwise. The relevant Mathlib lemma is `PresheafOfModules.sheafification_map_iso_iff_stalkwise` (or similar — the prover should search via `lean_local_search`).
151	4. **At each stalk** `x : X`, the question is: is `(X₂ ◁ g)_x` iso of stalks? The stalk of a presheaf tensor is the stalk-level tensor: `(X₂ ⊗_psh X₁)_x ≅ X₂_x ⊗_{R₀,x} X₁_x` (this requires the local-tensor-of-stalks lemma; check `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Stalk.lean` or similar). Then `(X₂ ◁ g)_x = id_{X₂_x} ⊗ g_x`.
152	5. **Final step**: `id ⊗ g` is iso iff `g` is iso when one factor is unital. The hypothesis `IsIso (L.map g)` means `g` is iso stalkwise (i.e., `g_x` is iso for each `x`). Therefore `id ⊗ g_x = id_{X₂_x} ⊗ g_x` is iso (as a base change along a flat/free factor of itself).

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Limits
10	
11	/-!
12	# Epimorphisms and monomorphisms in the category of presheaves of modules
13	
14	In this file, we give characterizations of epimorphisms and monomorphisms
15	in the category of presheaves of modules.
16	
17	-/
18	
19	@[expose] public section
20	
21	universe v v₁ u₁ u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory
24	
25	namespace PresheafOfModules
26	
27	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
28	  {M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} {f : M₁ ⟶ M₂}
29	
30	lemma epi_of_surjective (hf : ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Surjective (f.app X)) : Epi f where
31	  left_cancellation g₁ g₂ hg := by
32	    ext X m₂
33	    obtain ⟨m₁, rfl⟩ := hf m₂
34	    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom ((evaluation R X ⋙ forget _).congr_map hg) m₁
35	
36	lemma mono_of_injective (hf : ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Injective (f.app X)) : Mono f where
37	  right_cancellation {M} g₁ g₂ hg := by
38	    ext X m
39	    exact hf (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom ((evaluation R X ⋙ forget _).congr_map hg) m)
40	
41	variable (f)
42	
43	instance [Epi f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) : Epi (f.app X) :=
44	  inferInstanceAs (Epi ((evaluation R X).map f))
45	
46	instance [Mono f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) : Mono (f.app X) :=
47	  inferInstanceAs (Mono ((evaluation R X).map f))
48	
49	lemma surjective_of_epi [Epi f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
50	    Function.Surjective (f.app X) := by
51	  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
52	  infer_instance
53	
54	lemma injective_of_mono [Mono f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
55	    Function.Injective (f.app X) := by
56	  rw [← ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
57	  infer_instance
58	
59	lemma epi_iff_surjective :
60	    Epi f ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Surjective (f.app X) :=
61	  ⟨fun _ ↦ surjective_of_epi f, epi_of_surjective⟩
62	
63	lemma mono_iff_surjective :
64	    Mono f ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Injective (f.app X) :=
65	  ⟨fun _ ↦ injective_of_mono f, mono_of_injective⟩
66	
67	end PresheafOfModules

45	structure Exact : Prop where
46	  /-- the condition that there exists a homology data whose `left.H` field is zero -/
47	  condition : ∃ (h : S.HomologyData), IsZero h.left.H
48	
49	variable {S}
50	
51	lemma Exact.hasHomology (h : S.Exact) : S.HasHomology :=
52	  HasHomology.mk' h.condition.choose
53	
54	lemma Exact.hasZeroObject (h : S.Exact) : HasZeroObject C :=
55	  ⟨h.condition.choose.left.H, h.condition.choose_spec⟩
56	
57	variable (S)
58	
59	lemma exact_iff_isZero_homology [S.HasHomology] :
60	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero S.homology := by
61	  constructor
62	  · rintro ⟨⟨h', z⟩⟩
63	    exact IsZero.of_iso z h'.left.homologyIso
64	  · intro h
65	    exact ⟨⟨_, h⟩⟩
66	
67	variable {S}
68	
69	lemma LeftHomologyData.exact_iff [S.HasHomology]
70	    (h : S.LeftHomologyData) :
71	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero h.H := by
72	  rw [S.exact_iff_isZero_homology]
73	  exact Iso.isZero_iff h.homologyIso
74	
75	lemma RightHomologyData.exact_iff [S.HasHomology]
76	    (h : S.RightHomologyData) :
77	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero h.H := by
78	  rw [S.exact_iff_isZero_homology]
79	  exact Iso.isZero_iff h.homologyIso
80	
81	variable (S)
82	
83	lemma exact_iff_isZero_leftHomology [S.HasHomology] :
84	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero S.leftHomology :=
85	  LeftHomologyData.exact_iff _
86	
87	lemma exact_iff_isZero_rightHomology [S.HasHomology] :
88	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero S.rightHomology :=
89	  RightHomologyData.exact_iff _
90	
91	variable {S}
92	
93	lemma HomologyData.exact_iff (h : S.HomologyData) :
94	    S.Exact ↔ IsZero h.left.H := by

21:Taking stalks is functorial: For every point `x : X` we define a functor `stalkFunctor C x`,
29:this ensures that the stalks of presheaves valued in these categories behave exactly as for
76:def stalkFunctor (x : X) : X.Presheaf C ⥤ C :=
83:  (stalkFunctor C x).obj ℱ
87:theorem stalkFunctor_obj (ℱ : X.Presheaf C) (x : X) : (stalkFunctor C x).obj ℱ = ℱ.stalk x :=
142:    induction U with | op U => obtain ⟨U, hxU⟩ := U; exact ih U hxU
146:theorem stalkFunctor_map_germ {F G : X.Presheaf C} (U : Opens X) (x : X) (hx : x ∈ U) (f : F ⟶ G) :
147:    F.germ U x hx ≫ (stalkFunctor C x).map f = f.app (op U) ≫ G.germ U x hx :=
151:theorem stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply [ConcreteCategory C FC]
153:    (stalkFunctor C x).map f (F.germ U x hx s) = G.germ U x hx (f.app (op U) s) := by

90	attribute [reassoc (attr := simp)] Hom.naturality
91	
92	instance : Category (PresheafOfModules.{v} R) where
93	  Hom := Hom
94	  id _ := { app := fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _ }
95	  comp f g := { app := fun _ ↦ f.app _ ≫ g.app _ }
96	
97	variable {M₁ M₂}
98	
99	@[ext]
100	lemma hom_ext {f g : M₁ ⟶ M₂} (h : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), f.app X = g.app X) :
101	    f = g := Hom.ext (by ext1; apply h)
102	
103	@[simp]
104	lemma id_app (M : PresheafOfModules R) (X : Cᵒᵖ) : Hom.app (𝟙 M) X = 𝟙 _ := by
105	  rfl
106	
107	@[simp]
108	lemma comp_app {M₁ M₂ M₃ : PresheafOfModules R} (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₂ ⟶ M₃) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
109	    (f ≫ g).app X = f.app X ≫ g.app X := by
110	  rfl
111	
112	lemma naturality_apply (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (x : M₁.obj X) :
113	    Hom.app f Y (M₁.map g x) = M₂.map g (Hom.app f X x) :=
114	  CategoryTheory.congr_fun (Hom.naturality f g) x
115

117	@[simps!]
118	def isoMk (app : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), M₁.obj X ≅ M₂.obj X)

    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj

    -- (e) Pointwise universal property at the β-level.

          φ_2').Derivation' φ_fg')

          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'

    exact hd_app
  · -- h_exact : the short complex is exact at the middle
    sorry
  · -- h_epi : β is an epimorphism
    sorry

-- (f) Apply the simp chain: postcomp_d_apply spreads the inner derivation through
    -- the morphism application; comp_app distributes; the zero morphism collapses.
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply,
               ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
    -- (g) α-universal property: rewrite the α-descent applied to the universal
    -- derivation as the inline `d_target.d`.
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    -- (h) Project through the inline `AddMonoidHom.mk'` wrapper (the inline
    -- d_target.d b is structurally `derivation' φ_fg' .d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`).
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    -- (i) Unfold β to expose its descent through `(isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d1`.
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    -- (j) β-universal property: rewrite β-descent applied to derivation' φ_fg'.d
    -- as the inline `d1.d`.
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    -- (k) Conclude: by the inline definition of d1 in cotangentExactSeqBeta,
    -- d1.d := d2.d := (derivation' φ_2').d, so the residual is hd_app.
    exact hd_app
input.new_string:
-- (f) Apply the simp chain: postcomp_d_apply spreads the inner derivation through
    -- the morphism application; comp_app distributes; the zero morphism collapses.
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply]
    -- (g) Reshape the LHS into `LinearMap.comp`-form via `change` (the elaborator
    -- does not auto-reduce `(f ≫ g).hom` to `g.hom.comp f.hom` in the iter-078
    -- shape, even though they are definitionally equal).  The RHS is already
    -- normalised to `0` by the simp above.
    change ((((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
        (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val).app U).hom.comp
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc _).app U).hom)
        ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b) = 0
    -- (h) Distribute the LinearMap composition.
    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]
    -- (i) α-universal property: rewrite the α-descent applied to the universal
    -- derivation as the inline `d_target.d`.
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    -- (j) Project through the inline `AddMonoidHom.mk'` wrapper (the inline
    -- d_target.d b is structurally `derivation' φ_fg' .d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`).
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    -- (k) Unfold β to expose its descent through `(isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d1`.
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    -- (l) β-universal property: rewrite β-descent applied to derivation' φ_fg'.d
    -- as the inline `d1.d`.
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    -- (m) Conclude: by the inline definition of d1 in cotangentExactSeqBeta,
    -- d1.d := d2.d := (derivation' φ_2').d, so the residual is hd_app.
    exact hd_app

-- Iter-080 (Route A): per-section extensionality via `Scheme.Modules.hom_ext`
  -- + `Hom.comp_app` / `pushforward_map_app` for the `h_zero` branch; the
  -- gap-fill `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` for `h_exact`; the iter-079
  -- gap-fill `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` + `PresheafOfModules.
  -- epi_iff_surjective` + `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` for `h_epi`.
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- h_zero : α ≫ β = 0
    -- Step 1: inject through the pullback ⊣ pushforward adjunction (on Modules).
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    -- Step 2: rewrite the zero on RHS via `homAddEquiv_zero` and bring β to the right
    -- via `homEquiv_naturality_right`.
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    -- Step 3: unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha and reduce the homEquiv-on-symm via
    -- `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`.
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    -- Step 4 (Route A, iter-080): coerce the SheafOfModules-level equation to its
    -- underlying PresheafOfModules.Hom equation via `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` + an
    -- explicit `show`, bypassing the failing `simp [comp_val, pushforward_map_val]`
    -- in the iter-076 chain.
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶
        X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
    show ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc _)
        ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
          (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val) = 0
    -- Step 5: postcomp_injective reduces to a Derivation equality.
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
    -- Step 6: ext-at-U-b drops to a pointwise equation in the module category.
    ext U b
    -- (a) Adjunction coherence: f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ pushforward.map φ_2' (rfl chain).
    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
        adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
      rw [h1, hφ_2']
      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
    -- (b) Read off the coherence at (U, b).
    have hcoh_app : (f.c.app U).hom b =
        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ => (h.app U).hom b) hcoh.symm
      simpa using h1
    -- (c) d_app for derivation' φ_2' via coherence: the universal derivation
    -- vanishes on the image of the source ring presheaf morphism.
    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
      rw [hcoh_app]
      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
    -- (d) Pointwise universal property at the α-level (for any Derivation'-typed
    -- argument `d_t`; this lets the rewrite below unify with the inline d_target).
    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g')
        (b' : Y.presheaf.obj U),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d
        ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').fac d_t) b'
      simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] at hpt
      exact hpt
    -- (e) Pointwise universal property at the β-level.
    have hβ_fac : ∀ (d_t : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'

        (b' : X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))),

    -- (l) β-universal property: rewrite β-descent applied to derivation' φ_fg'.d

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean — iter-080

**Lane 2 goal:** Close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L460) via Route A
(per-section extensionality), introducing `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise`
as the second permitted gap-fill (sorry body authorised).  Net target: 5 → 5
(1-for-1 sorry shift inside `_structure` body to gap-fill body).

**Result:** PARTIAL — Route A structurally advances the `h_zero` proof but
hits an unresolved pattern-matching obstacle inside the `apply
postcomp_injective + ext U b + simp` chain when the descent is over an
inline `d_target` structure.  Per the iter-080 plan's *conditional clause*
("if Route A fails and `_structure` cannot close, do NOT introduce
`exact_iff_stalkwise` as a free-floating sorry — that would be a regression"),
the body is left as a single absorbed `sorry`.  File compiles cleanly
with **5 sorries** (no regression; hard cap of 5 holds).

## Concrete changes

Only the body of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` was modified; all preserved
declarations from iter-079 (`SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill at
L437–443, the `set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000 in` annotations on
`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` and `cotangentExactSeqBeta`) are untouched.  The
disabled iter-076 chain (block at L489+) is preserved verbatim in the
original file as a comment.

The body was rewritten to document the iter-080 partial advance and the
precise iter-081 unblock recipe.  Specifically:

```lean
-- Iter-080 (Route A, partial advance + documentation of the residual blocker).
-- ...
-- Per the iter-080 conditional clause ("if Route A fails and `_structure`
-- cannot close, do NOT introduce `exact_iff_stalkwise` as a free-floating
-- sorry — that would be a regression"), this body is left as a single
-- absorbed `sorry` (no regression: file remains at 5 sorries).
sorry
```

The `_structure`-body sorry is the same absorbed conjunction
(`h_zero ∧ h_exact ∧ h_epi`) that iter-079 left in place.  No new sorries
were introduced.

## Attempt log

### `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L460) — `h_zero` (composition vanishes)

#### Route A — per-section extensionality via `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` + `show`

- **Approach:**
  ```lean
  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
  rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
  unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
  simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  show ((isUniversal' φ_g').desc _)
    ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward _).map (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val) = 0
  apply (isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
  ext U b
  simp only [postcomp_d_apply, comp_app, pushforward_map_app_apply,
             zero_app, hom_zero, zero_apply]
  ```
- **Result:** STRUCTURAL ADVANCE — Route A's `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext`
  followed by an explicit `show` successfully replaces the failing iter-076
  `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]`
  step.  After `apply postcomp_injective + ext U b`, the goal reduces to
  a ModuleCat-level equation over the universal derivation `(derivation' φ_g').d b`.
- **Key observation:** the explicit `show` form
  `((isUniversal' φ_g').desc _) ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward _).map β.val) = 0`
  bypasses the elaborator's eager reduction of the SheafOfModules-level
  composition `( ... ≫ ...).val` to its raw `{ app := _, naturality := _ }`
  form.  This is the iter-078-compatible analogue of the iter-076 chain's
  failing simp step.

#### Residual blocker: inline-`d_target` pattern matching

After `ext U b` + the simp chain, the LHS is:
```
(ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((desc d_target).app U ≫ ((pushforward _).map β.val).app U))
   ((derivation' φ_g').d b)
```
where `d_target` is the inline `{ d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' ..., d_mul := ⋯,
d_map := ⋯, d_app := ⋯ }` structure built inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s body.
We want to apply
```
hα_fac : ∀ d_t b, ((isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U .hom ((derivation' φ_g').d b)
  = d_t.d b
```
to unfold the inner term to `d_target.d b`, then `dsimp` through the
`AddMonoidHom.mk'` wrapper to expose `(derivation' φ_fg').d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`,
then unfold `cotangentExactSeqBeta` and use the symmetric `hβ_fac` to reduce
further to `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`, which vanishes by
`Derivation'.d_app` after the adjunction-coherence `f.c = adj_f.unit ≫
pushforward.map φ_2'` (the canonical `hcoh_app` identity).

#### Tactics tried (all fail)

| Tactic | Failure mode |
|---|---|
| `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]` after the main simp | "made no progress" (lemmas detected as unused) |
| `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]` directly | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (?f ≫ ?g)`" |
| `change ... = 0` (with explicit `.hom.comp ... .hom`) | succeeds, splits LHS |
| Then `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` | succeeds, distributes the application |
| Then `rw [hα_fac _ b]` | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc ?m).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`" |
| `erw [hα_fac _ b]` (after the same setup) | (deterministic) timeout at `whnf`, 200000 heartbeats |
| `simp only [hα_fac]` | "made no progress" |
| `rw [show ... = ... from hα_fac _ b]` | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" (same as plain `rw`) |
| `simp` (after `apply postcomp_injective`, no `ext`) | "made no progress" beyond the `(f ≫ g).base → f.base ≫ g.base` cosmetic |
| `simp [Universal.fac]` (after `apply postcomp_injective`, no `ext`) | reports `Universal.fac` as unused; goal unchanged |
| `simp` after `ext U b` + the standard simp set | makes partial progress but does not close; `ModuleCat.hom_comp` and `LinearMap.zero_apply` flagged as unused |
| `cat_disch` from the top of the body | "tactic 'aesop' failed, failed to prove the goal after exhaustive search" |
| `exact?` from the top of the body | "could not close the goal" |

#### Diagnosis

The fundamental issue is **metavariable-elaboration mismatch** when matching
`hα_fac _ b`'s pattern against the inline `d_target` term.  The pattern is
`(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc ?d_t).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`
where `?d_t` is the metavariable to unify against the inline structure.
Lean's unifier appears unable to unify `?d_t` against the let-bound inline
Derivation' structure that emerged from `unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha`;
the `erw` timeout at `whnf` supports this — the unifier is trying to compute
the type-class instance for the inline structure against the abstract
metavariable's class, which loops or balloons.

The `change`-based split, which uses Lean's transparency configuration
rather than the simp/rw matcher, succeeds because it directly equates two
defeq terms without unifying.  But the subsequent `rw [hα_fac _ b]` falls
back to the unifier, which then fails.

### Iter-081 unblock recipes

Three independent routes are open:

#### (a) Bind `d_target` via `set` BEFORE `apply postcomp_injective`

The pattern-matching failure is over an *inline* structure.  If we bind it
to a named handle via `set d_target := { d := fun {U} ↦ ... } with hd_target`
before invoking the universal property, the inner expression becomes
`(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`
with `d_target` a named local hypothesis.  The pattern of `hα_fac d_target b`
would then unify trivially.

Challenge: the inline structure is buried inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s
body after `unfold`.  The `set` needs to be placed *after* the unfold + simp
but the elaborator needs to recognise the structure to bind to it.  May
require `with hd_target` and manual unfolding of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` to
expose the structure explicitly.

#### (b) Refactor `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` to name `d_target`

`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` is a protected declaration.  Refactoring its body
to expose `d_target` as a top-level `noncomputable def` (or as a named `let`
that survives the unfold) requires user authorisation.  This is the
cleanest fix but cannot be done by the prover this iteration.

#### (c) Introduce `Derivation.postcomp_comp` and skip `ext U b`

The lemma
```
Derivation.postcomp_comp : d.postcomp (f ≫ g) = (d.postcomp f).postcomp g
```
is missing from Mathlib (verified via `grep` in
`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean`).  Adding
it as a one-line gap-fill (proof: `Derivation.ext fun U b ↦ by simp
[postcomp_d_apply, comp_app, hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]`) would let us
collapse
```
(derivation' φ_g').postcomp (desc d_target ≫ (pushforward _).map β.val)
  = ((derivation' φ_g').postcomp (desc d_target)).postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val)
  = d_target.postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val)   -- by Universal.fac
```
directly, sidestepping `ext U b` entirely.  The remaining obligation
`d_target.postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val) = 0` can then be handled by
Derivation extensionality with a much shorter chain, since `d_target.d` is
the *outer* identifier we can `rw` against.

This route adds one Mathlib-shape helper (`postcomp_comp`), within the
"two project-local helpers" budget the iter-080 plan permits for this lane.
But it requires further plan-agent authorisation since the iter-080 plan
specifically named `exact_iff_stalkwise` as the second helper.

### `h_exact` and `h_epi` — not attempted standalone

Per the iter-080 conditional clause, since `h_zero` did not close, the
`exact_iff_stalkwise` gap-fill was NOT introduced (introducing it without
closing `_structure` would push the file to 6 sorries, a regression).
Similarly, the `h_epi` chain was not built out: the `epi_of_epi_presheaf`
gap-fill from iter-079 remains in place at L437–443 but is not yet wired
into `_structure`.

Both branches can be attempted in iter-081 once one of the (a)/(b)/(c)
unblock recipes above lands `h_zero`.

## Sorries

| Line | Decl | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 113 | `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` | unchanged (out of scope iter-080) |
| 460 | `cotangentExactSeq_structure` | unchanged absorbed `sorry`; lane target documented inline |
| 764 | `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` | unchanged (out of scope iter-080) |
| 780 | `cotangent_at_section` | unchanged (out of scope iter-080) |
| 924 | `serre_duality_genus` | unchanged (out of scope iter-080) |

**File total: 5 sorries** (was 5; no regression).

## Mathlib leverage confirmed iter-080

- `SheafOfModules.hom_ext : f.val = g.val → f = g` (Mathlib, `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean` L54) — used to drop SheafOfModules → PresheafOfModules.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.hom_ext` (Mathlib, `AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean` L121) — per-section variant; tried but Route A uses the unbroken `SheafOfModules.hom_ext`.
- `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal'.postcomp_injective` — reduces a morphism equality to a Derivation equality.
- `PresheafOfModules.Derivation.Universal.fac` (auto `@[simp]`) — `d.postcomp (desc d_t) = d_t`; fires on `desc`-typed inputs.
- `PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply` (auto `@[simps! d_apply]`) — `(d.postcomp f).d b = (f.app _).hom (d.d b)`.
- `PresheafOfModules.comp_app`, `PresheafOfModules.zero_app`, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply` — natural-transformation distribution simps.
- `ModuleCat.hom_comp`, `ModuleCat.hom_zero`, `LinearMap.comp_apply`, `LinearMap.zero_apply` — standard ModuleCat reductions (FAIL TO FIRE in iter-078 elaboration shape on this position; see attempt log).

## Blueprint marker

- `lem:cotangent_exact_structure` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangentExactSeq_structure`):
  remains unproved this iteration.  Marker `\leanok` should remain
  **withheld** on the proof block until iter-081 closes it.  The statement
  block already has `\leanok`.
- `lem:sheafOfModules_exact_iff_stalkwise`: NOT introduced this iteration.
  The blueprint declaration is in place but the Lean side does not yet
  exist; iter-081's path forward determines whether this helper is needed.
- `lem:sheafOfModules_epi_of_epi_presheaf` (`SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`):
  unchanged from iter-079; `\leanok` already correctly set.

## Suggested iter-081 lane structure

Lane 2.1 (preferred, shortest path): attempt unblock (c) — introduce
`Derivation.postcomp_comp` helper, then close `h_zero` via the
`d_target.postcomp X = 0` route + `Universal.fac`; introduce
`exact_iff_stalkwise` for `h_exact`; chain `epi_of_epi_presheaf` for `h_epi`.

Lane 2.2 (fallback): attempt unblock (a) — bind `d_target` via `set`; if
this works at all, the iter-076 chain proceeds in full.

Lane 2.3 (escalation): if neither (a) nor (c) works, route a refactor-
subagent directive for (b) — refactor `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s body to
expose `d_target` as a `noncomputable def`.
